Developer Guide: Fusion for JavaThis guide explains how to implement the server-side of the Fusion 50/50 Standard. It focuses on using HybridEngine for crypto and AsyncResult for non-blocking performance.1. PrerequisitesJava 17 or higher (Required for record types and sealed interfaces).No external dependencies (We use standard JDK java.security for ECC and custom math for Lattice).2. The Hybrid EngineThe HybridEngine class is your main entry point. It manages the state of both ECDH and Lattice keys.Initialization:import com.fusion.crypto.HybridEngine;

// Initialize strictly once per session context
HybridEngine engine = new HybridEngine();
engine.init(); // Auto-generates keys with Telemetry tracking
3. Handling a Client RequestWhen a React client sends their public key bundle, you must encapsulate a secret back to them.// 1. Receive keys (Mocking the DTO deserialization)
var clientKeys = new HybridEngine.PublicKeyBundle(
    clientEccKey,   // java.security.PublicKey
    clientKyberKey  // HybridEngine.MiniKyber.PublicKey
);

// 2. Encapsulate
// This automatically runs the 50/50 logic and hashes the result
// Returns a HybridResult containing the SessionKey and the Ciphertext
var result = engine.encapsulate(clientKeys);

// 3. Send 'result.ciphertext()' back to the client
// 4. Use 'result.sessionKey()' to decrypt their message
4. Using AsyncResultCryptographic operations (especially Lattice matrix multiplication) can be CPU intensive. Use AsyncResult to prevent blocking your main Web Server thread (Tomcat/Jetty).import com.fusion.core.AsyncResult;

// 1. Create an async task
AsyncResult.from("ProcessHandshake", () -> {
    // This runs in a separate thread pool
    return engine.encapsulate(clientKeys);
})
.toFuture()
.thenAccept(result -> {
    // 2. Handle the Result safely
    if (result.isSuccess()) {
        sendResponse(result.unwrap().ciphertext());
    } else {
        logSecurityIncident(result.unwrapErr());
    }
});
5. Security GuardrailsAlways validate input before processing. The Guard class is your firewall against Downgrade Attacks.import com.fusion.core.Guard;

public void handleRequest(Object payload) {
    // Throws SecurityException immediately if the payload isn't a valid Hybrid Bundle
    // This prevents "Null Pointer" exploits deeper in the crypto stack
    Guard.validateHybridStructure(payload.classical, payload.quantum);
    
    // Logic proceeds only if both keys exist...
}
